accepted_attempt.summary: reader enforces normalized safe-path and extension invariants on bounded adversarial cases

constraints: Reject absolute paths and any normalized path outside the base directory.
Reject files whose normalized path does not end in .txt.
Do not touch the real filesystem; use only the provided file_map.

counterexample.description: Candidates must stay within the provided runtime and cannot import modules.
counterexample.title: forbidden_import

failing_attempt.summary: imports are not allowed in candidate tools

casefiles.name: Local Context Reader
casefiles.primary_invariant: A request may only read `.txt` files that remain inside the declared safe base directory after normalization.
casefiles.signature: read_local_context(base_dir: str, requested_path: str, file_map: dict[str, str]) -> str
casefiles.task: Normalize a user-requested relative path, reject traversal or non-.txt access, and read the content from the provided in-memory file map.
casefiles.thesis: The reader must treat path normalization as a safety boundary rather than a convenience feature.
casefiles.verification_lane: AST guard plus bounded adversarial path coverage over normalized in-memory file access.

accepted_attempt.summary: query runner preserves the SELECT-only grammar on bounded safe and adversarial examples

constraints: Reject DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, DROP, ALTER, CREATE, PRAGMA, semicolons, and unknown columns.
Treat the input `rows` as the only table.

counterexample.description: Candidates must stay within the provided runtime and cannot import modules.
counterexample.title: forbidden_import

failing_attempt.summary: imports are not allowed in candidate tools

casefiles.name: Bounded SQL Querier
casefiles.primary_invariant: Only a safe SELECT grammar is allowed; mutating SQL or multi-statement queries must be rejected.
casefiles.signature: run_bounded_sql(query: str, rows: list[dict[str, int]]) -> list[dict[str, int]]
casefiles.task: Execute a tiny SELECT-only SQL fragment over an in-memory table of rows.
casefiles.thesis: A tiny SELECT-only fragment is easier to verify and still useful for agent retrieval.
casefiles.verification_lane: AST guard plus oracle-matched execution over safe and unsafe query fixtures.

accepted_attempt.summary: safe request did not match mocked API oracle output

constraints: Reject localhost, private IPs, bare IP hosts, and non-HTTPS schemes.
Only the GET method is allowed.
Look up the final response in the provided responses map using the key '<METHOD> <URL>'.

counterexample.description: Candidates must stay within the provided runtime and cannot import modules.
counterexample.title: forbidden_import

failing_attempt.summary: imports are not allowed in candidate tools

casefiles.name: Safe API Caller
casefiles.primary_invariant: Only HTTPS GET requests to explicitly allowlisted public hosts are permitted.
casefiles.signature: safe_api_call(url: str, method: str, allowlist: list[str], responses: dict[str, str]) -> str
casefiles.task: Validate an outbound API request against a strict allowlist and return a mocked response body.
casefiles.thesis: The network boundary belongs in the tool contract, not in the model's intentions.
casefiles.verification_lane: URL policy oracle covering scheme, host, IP, and method restrictions.

accepted_attempt.code:
def append_only_log(existing: str, new_entry: str, max_bytes: int = 4096) -> str:
    # Check for NUL bytes in the new entry
    if '\x00' in new_entry:
        raise ValueError("New entry contains NUL bytes, which are not allowed.")
    
    # Prepare the separator if needed
    separator = '\n' if existing and not existing.endswith('\n') else ''
    
    # Construct the new log
    new_log = existing + separator + new_entry
    
    # Check if the new log exceeds the maximum allowed bytes
    if len(new_log.encode('utf-8')) > max_bytes:
        raise ValueError("Appending the new entry would exceed the maximum allowed size.")
    
    return new_log
accepted_attempt.summary: logger preserves prefix monotonicity and bounded growth on bounded adversarial cases

constraints: Preserve the full previous log exactly as a prefix.
If the existing log is non-empty and does not end with a newline, insert one before the new entry.
Reject outputs whose byte length would exceed max_bytes.

counterexample.description: Oversized writes and NUL bytes should be rejected.
counterexample.title: invalid_append_accepted

casefiles.name: Append-Only Logger
casefiles.primary_invariant: The previous log must remain a prefix of the new log; size must never exceed the configured bound.
casefiles.signature: append_only_log(existing: str, new_entry: str, max_bytes: int = 4096) -> str
casefiles.task: Append a new log entry without mutating existing bytes and enforce a hard output-size cap.
casefiles.thesis: Scratchpad memory is safest when writes become monotonic state transitions.
casefiles.verification_lane: Prefix-preservation checks and bounded byte-budget enforcement.

accepted_attempt.summary: masker removes all configured PII classes on bounded fixture coverage

constraints: Replace matches with stable placeholders rather than deleting all surrounding context.
Handle multiple PII categories in one string.
Do not introduce new digits that recreate a banned pattern.

counterexample.description: Candidates must stay within the provided runtime and cannot import modules.
counterexample.title: forbidden_import

casefiles.name: PII Masker
casefiles.primary_invariant: Masked output must not contain emails, US phone numbers, SSNs, or 16-digit card numbers recognized by the repository regex suite.
casefiles.task: Redact common PII patterns before text is sent to an LLM.
casefiles.thesis: Sanitization should fail closed on known high-risk PII classes before context leaves the host.
casefiles.verification_lane: Regex-backed fixture suite with stable placeholder expectations.

accepted_attempt.summary: imports are not allowed in candidate tools

constraints: Support integers, variables, parentheses, unary minus, +, -, *, and //.
Reject function calls, attributes, exponentiation, unknown variables, and expressions with more than max_nodes AST nodes.
Return the same result as the reference interpreter on bounded inputs.

counterexample.description: Candidates must stay within the provided runtime and cannot import modules.
counterexample.title: forbidden_import

casefiles.name: Compute-Bounded Evaluator
casefiles.primary_invariant: Only a bounded whitelist of arithmetic AST nodes is allowed, and evaluation must reject unsupported or oversized expressions.
casefiles.signature: bounded_evaluate(expression: str, variables: dict[str, int], max_nodes: int = 32) -> int
casefiles.task: Evaluate a tiny arithmetic expression language with explicit resource bounds.
casefiles.thesis: If the evaluator language is tiny, the verifier can exhaustively compare semantics on bounded cases.
casefiles.verification_lane: AST whitelist plus bounded arithmetic equivalence against a reference interpreter.

tagline: CEGIS for synthesizing safe agent tools under explicit environment invariants.
timeline: Specify a micro-scoped tool and its primary invariant.
Generate a candidate implementation through the synthesis interface.
Run AST guards and bounded executable verifiers.
Feed the counterexample back into the next generation attempt.
Accept only the first candidate that satisfies the bounded verifier suite.
title: VeriTool
